behavior_in_task_description.reason: All test-asserted specifics — variable name LAST_THEME, exact string 'let LAST_THEME = app.state.theme', 'app.onChangeEmitter.on', 'app.state.theme !== LAST_THEME', 'unsubOnChange()', the FIXME comment text, file path, and commands like 'yarn test' — are explicitly mentioned in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: instruction.md prescribes the exact implementation: declare 'let LAST_THEME = app.state.theme', update it at beginning of updateWysiwygStyle(), use 'app.onChangeEmitter.on(...)' with 'app.state.theme !== LAST_THEME', name the variable 'unsubOnChange', call 'unsubOnChange()' in cleanup, and include a specific FIXME comment. This is a complete solution specification, not a problem description.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert exact strings like 'let LAST_THEME = app.state.theme', 'app.onChangeEmitter.on', 'app.state.theme !== LAST_THEME', 'unsubOnChange()', and the exact FIXME comment. An alternative correct fix using different variable names (e.g., 'previousTheme'), different subscription mechanisms, or different comment text would fail these string-matching tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: 5 of 10 tests (test_last_theme_variable_exists, test_last_theme_updated_in_style_function, test_onchange_emitter_subscription_exists, test_unsub_onchange_cleanup_exists, test_fixme_comment_present) are purely string-matching via 'assert X in content' with no code execution. These fail-to-pass tests exclusively check text presence, not behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: All 5 fail-to-pass tests can be satisfied by simply appending the required strings as comments to the file without implementing any actual functionality. E.g., adding '// let LAST_THEME = app.state.theme' or dead code containing these strings would pass the substring checks.

behavior_in_tests.reason: instruction.md requires that 'text color in the editor should immediately update to match the theme' and 'apply the appropriate dark mode filter'. No test verifies actual color updating behavior — test_theme_change_wysiwyg_test silently passes if the test doesn't exist in the codebase.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive docstrings, clearly separated into fail-to-pass and pass-to-pass categories, and each tests a distinct aspect of the implementation.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task does not produce structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, commands, and string literals in instruction.md and tests are consistent and free of typos.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md specifies the exact file (textWysiwyg.tsx), exact variable names (LAST_THEME, unsubOnChange), exact API calls (app.onChangeEmitter.on), exact placement (module-level, beginning of updateWysiwygStyle), and exact comment text. This goes far beyond identifying the buggy module.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only task, not a config edit task.
